data.title: NewLake signals dividend resilience with 79% payout ratio amid tenant default risk
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: NewLake Capital Partners (NLCP) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Gordon F. DuGan, Independent Chairman, stated that "NewLake delivered solid performance in a challenging cannabis landscape." He highlighted the second quarter dividend of $0.43 per share with an AFFO payout ratio of 79%, which he described as "just below our targeted range of 80% to 90%." DuGan addressed an emerging default with a tenant representing approximately 5.9% of annualized base rent, reassuring that “we actively enforce our rights under the lease agreements and remain committed to maximizing shareholder value in these distressed situations.” He emphasized the company's flexibility, saying, “In an environment where some peers are distributing a larger share of earnings, NewLake's lower payout ratio gives us valuable flexibility to manage potential challenges.”

  • Anthony Coniglio, President and CEO, explained, “While second quarter results were in line with expectations, we are disappointed to see that future quarters may be adversely impacted by the restructuring activities at Ayr Wellness.” Coniglio detailed that Ayr Wellness, which leases two properties totaling 5.9% of annualized base rent, has not paid August rent and that NewLake holds approximately 3.5 months of security deposit for each property. He stated, “We intend to fully enforce our rights under the lease agreements and aggressively work to replace any defaulting tenants.”

  • Coniglio shared that all rent due during the quarter was collected, acquisitions included a dispensary in Ohio for $875,000, and a deed-for-deed property swap with Curaleaf was executed. He noted active marketing of a recently vacated Massachusetts facility and the addition of butter as a new tenant following their acquisition of a Connecticut dispensary.

  • Lisa Meyer, CFO, stated, “In the second quarter of 2025, our portfolio generated total revenue of $12.9 million, a 3.8% increase year-over- year, reflecting the quality of our portfolio.” She added, “Net income attributable to common shareholders for the 3 months ended June 30, 2025, was $7.3 million or $0.35 per diluted share. Adjusted funds from operations totaled $11.5 million for the quarter, representing a 4% increase year-over-year.”

Outlook

  • Coniglio indicated subdued demand for cultivation projects in the second half of 2025, stating, “I do expect a subdued level of demand for cultivation projects in the second half of '25 versus prior years.” He reinforced that, apart from Ayr, all tenants are current with rent through August. Meyer commented, “We have ample flexibility to execute on our business strategy and grow earnings by continuing to invest in quality assets.”

Financial Results

  • Meyer reported, “Revenue and AFFO were higher than last year, and our payout ratio was under 80%.” She noted the dividend continues to be “fully supported by the earnings power of our portfolio with a second quarter payout ratio of only 79%.” The company’s liquidity at quarter end included $432.2 million in gross real estate assets, $21.9 million in cash, and $82.4 million in credit facility capacity. The company’s debt to total gross assets was 1.6%, with no maturity until May 2027. A revenue reduction of approximately $0.017 per share per quarter is expected following the vacancy of the Massachusetts property.

Q&A

  • Rahul, Zuanic & Associates, asked about large-scale acquisition or build-out activity for the remainder of 2025. Coniglio responded that “the development of cultivation properties, for sure, is significantly less than previous years,” and expects subdued demand for cultivation in the second half of the year.

  • Rahul inquired about the $11 million in unfunded commitments with C3 Industries. Coniglio answered, “At this time, we're working with our tenant to evaluate options. And so for the time being, investors should expect that we'll continue our pause on funding that additional TI for the foreseeable future.”

  • Rahul asked if Acreage was current on rent. Coniglio confirmed, “Aside from what we've mentioned on this call with Ayr, all of our tenants are current through and including August rent.”

  • Rahul asked about MSOs’ openness to sale leaseback. Coniglio shared that “sale-leaseback transactions are an important tool for companies across all industries to fund those real estate capital needs... I do believe that MSOs are open to leasing and sale leasebacks.”

  • Michael Peterson asked about leverage and real estate asset valuation. Meyer clarified, “The real estate assets are recorded in accordance with GAAP. So when we acquire an asset, that asset is recorded on the balance sheet at its fair value, and then we don't fair value it going forward.”

  • Peterson questioned risks for shareholders given the payout ratio. Coniglio responded that the main risk is “around the tenancy and the collection of rent for this industry.” He added, “It's one of the reasons why our yield... is higher than what you would see for a typical REIT because the cannabis industry itself is more high risk and more volatile than other industries in the U.S.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral to slightly cautious, with persistent queries about tenant risk, leverage, and capital deployment, but no overt skepticism or negativity.

  • Management maintained a confident and pragmatic tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing risk management and financial flexibility. DuGan stated, “we actively enforce our rights under the lease agreements and remain committed to maximizing shareholder value in these distressed situations.”

  • Compared to the previous quarter, there were more direct questions about tenant defaults and risk, but management’s messaging remained consistent, focusing on stability, conservative payout, and adaptability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter introduced the new risk from Ayr Wellness’ restructuring and a potential rent default affecting 5.9% of annualized base rent. In Q1, no such default was disclosed.

  • The AFFO payout ratio improved from 84% in Q1 to 79% in Q2, indicating increased dividend coverage.

  • Management’s strategic tone remained cautious, but Q2 placed more emphasis on actively managing distressed tenants and maintaining dividend stability.

  • Analysts focused more on specific tenant risks in Q2, while Q1 questions were broader, including CapEx cycles and regulatory outlooks.

Risks and Concerns

  • An emerging tenant default with Ayr Wellness, representing 5.9% of annualized base rent, was highlighted, with management holding 3.5 months of security deposit and preparing to re-tenant the properties.

  • Revolutionary Clinics’ vacancy in Massachusetts is expected to reduce revenue by approximately $0.017 per share per quarter until re-leased.

  • Subdued demand for cultivation projects and continued industry headwinds were noted.

  • Management is monitoring C3 Industries’ Connecticut facility and is pausing additional funding pending further evaluation.

Final Takeaway

NewLake Capital Partners emphasized financial flexibility, a conservative payout ratio, and an active approach to managing tenant risks, particularly with the Ayr Wellness restructuring. The company reiterated its commitment to maintaining dividend coverage and shareholder value through prudent asset management and a strong balance sheet, even as sector headwinds and tenant-specific challenges persist.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tandem downgraded to Neutral at Piper Sandler on softer outlook
data.description:
Tandem Diabetes (NASDAQ:TNDM) was downgraded to Neutral from Overweight at Piper Sandler following the company’s updated guidance, reflecting increased uncertainty and a softer outlook in key areas.

While the company reported Q2 results above estimates, Piper Sandler analysts feel the domestic U.S. outlook has clearly softened, with management now forecasting flat renewals for next year, an indication of demand challenges in the largest market.

"The impact of the pipeline remains uncertain and the gross margin outlook is steep to us. Consequently, we are moving to the sidelines on the name," analysts added.

The shares of TNDM have tanked about 20% so far today after the company issued voluntary medical device correction for select t:slim X2 insulin pumps to address a potential speaker-related issue that can trigger an error resulting in a discontinuation of insulin delivery.

data.title: SA Asks: How could higher India tariffs impact drug stocks?
data.description:
India is a major supplier of prescription drugs, over-the-counter medications, and pharmaceutical ingredients to the United States. 

Recently, President Trump announced he intends to impose a 25% tariff on products imported from India, plus a 25% punitive tariff for the country continuing to buy Russian oil.  

So far, pharmaceuticals appear to be exempt from those tariffs. However, the president also recently announced that he plans to impose tariffs as high as 250% on imported pharmaceuticals as part of an effort to get foreign drugmakers to manufacture their products in the U.S. 

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Stephen Ayers and ALLKA Research how they think higher U.S. tariffs on Indian pharmaceutical products could impact stocks. 

Stephen Ayers: The India tariffs could hurt generics-focused Indian companies that derive a lot of their revenue from the U.S. market.  

A prime example is Dr. Reddy's Laboratories (NYSE:RDY), which specializes in generics and active pharmaceutical ingredients, or APIs. In fact, Dr. Reddy's is facing two major headwinds: tariffs on imports and a push by the U.S. government for more stringent intellectual property protections for innovator drug companies.  

As far as I am aware, for now, pharmaceuticals from India are exempt from tariffs. However, this could be later used as a negotiating tool to strike a deal that would weaken India-based generic manufacturers such as Dr. Reddy's. India is well-known for its manufacturing of low-cost generic medicines. It has been estimated that about half the generic drugs in the U.S. are from India.

Companies poised to, at least, indirectly benefit include large and established U.S. pharmaceutical companies like AbbVie (ABBV), Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) and Merck & Co. (NYSE:MRK). If IP laws are tightened in India, it would effectively delay generic competition for U.S. Big Pharma blockbuster drugs, extending monopoly pricing power and protecting billions in revenue.

For example, some blockbuster drugs, like Merck's Keytruda, generate tens of billions of dollars each year. So, even if generic manufacturers are delayed by just one more year, it could mean billions more in revenue for companies like Merck. 

ALLKA Research: Trump continues to play with fire by threatening to impose additional tariffs on India. 

As Indian companies account for 47% of generic drug prescriptions in the U.S., I believe that Trump's tariff policy will slow down EBITDA growth at key players such as Dr. Reddy's Laboratories (NYSE:RDY), Sun Pharma and Biocon in the short term. These three giants are also key suppliers of a wide range of low-cost generics, vaccines and biosimilars for such drugs as Johnson & Johnson's Stelara (JNJ), AbbVie's Humira (ABBV) and Novo Nordisk's NovoLog (NVO).  

If Trump goes all-in, which is unlikely, it will require Indian drugmakers to spend billions of rupees and at least three years to transfer production of active pharmaceutical ingredients, or APIs, to the U.S., which will, of course, harm Americans through higher prices and lead to temporary disruptions in the supply of vital drugs. 

Among the American stocks, I highlight Teva (NYSE:TEVA), Viatris (NASDAQ:VTRS), Perrigo (NYSE:PRGO) and Amneal (NASDAQ:AMRX), which are also dependent on the export of APIs from India to their production facilities in the U.S. 

As a result, I believe that despite the ongoing uncertainty on Wall Street regarding the Indian pharmaceutical industry, Trump will hesitate to toughen his stance so as not to provoke a new round of inflation, as well as to avoid creating a shortage of cheap copies of branded drugs.

data.title: Stablecoin issuer Paxos fined $26.5M by NYDFS over BUSD-tied compliance lapses
data.description:
The New York Department of Financial Services said Thursday it has fined stablecoin issuer Paxos Trust Company $26.5M for anti-money laundering deficiencies and compliance failures tied to its partnership previous alliance with crypto exchange Binance.

Paxos also agreed to invest an additional $22M to improve its compliance program and meet NYDFS standards.

In 2019, Paxos joined hands with Binance to issue the trading platform's dollar-pegged stablecoin BUSD (BUSD-USD). Four years later, though, NYDFS launched a probe into BUSD issuance, prompting Paxos to stop issuing the token.

The focus of Thursday's announcement was on the regulator's 2023 investigation, which "revealed that Paxos did not have appropriate controls in place to effectively monitor for significant illicit activity occurring at or through Binance, and failed to escalate red flags to Paxos’s senior management and its Board," it said in a statement.

Beyond Paxos's Binance-tied compliance failures, NYDFS found that for years Paxos operated an "unsophisticated" know your customer/customer due diligence program, allowing illicit players to open more than one account and stay undetected.

On top of that, "Paxos’s deficient transaction monitoring system also prevented the Company from detecting obvious patterns of money laundering, thus exacerbating its onboarding compliance deficiencies," NYDFS said.

data.title: Tegna reaffirms $900M-$1.1B free cash flow outlook through 2025 while accelerating digital and cost-cutting initiatives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TEGNA Inc. (TGNA) Q2 2025

Management View

  • President and CEO Michael F. Steib highlighted confidence in TEGNA's positioning, citing "strong local brands, high-quality local journalism, loyal audiences, deep roots with advertisers, healthy balance sheet and a terrific team" as strengths. He described positive regulatory momentum, referencing the Eighth U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals decision to vacate the FCC's top 4 prohibition rule and noted that "these developments are a significant step forward for our industry and for TEGNA's wide range of options in this evolving landscape."
  • Steib outlined five key priorities: building a world-class team, leveraging station strengths, deploying technology and AI, growing digital revenue, and cutting unnecessary spending. He announced the appointment of five new regional heads of content and a major expansion of local news streaming: "Just last month, we announced a major local news expansion adding dedicated 7 to 9 a.m. streaming programming in over 50 markets. That's more than 100 new hours of local news every single day."
  • He recognized the upcoming retirement of COO Lynn Beall, calling her "integral to supporting me over the past year and helping us craft a growth strategy that is already bearing fruit."
  • CFO Julie A. Heskett stated, "Our second quarter financial results exceeded our expectations, primarily driven by lower operating expenses, which came in better than our previously announced guidance range." She emphasized proactive cost management, digital revenue growth, and reaffirmed commitment to "building a more sustainable and growth-oriented future at TEGNA."

Outlook

  • Heskett reaffirmed adjusted free cash flow guidance of $900 million to $1.1 billion over 2024-2025. She reported, "We are lowering our full year 2025 interest expense guidance range to $160 million to $165 million, reflecting the $250 million par value partial redemption of our senior notes due in March."
  • For Q3, Heskett guided, "We expect total company revenue to decline 18% to 20% year-over-year, in line with expectations, given the cyclical nature of our business specifically the shift from an even year with significant political and Summer Olympic advertising to an odd year without those revenue drivers. We expect non-GAAP operating expenses to decline 2% to 3% year-over-year."

Financial Results

  • Heskett reported, "Total company revenue for the second quarter decreased 5% year-over-year to $675 million, in line with our outlook range of down 4% to 7%."
  • Advertising and marketing services (AMS) revenue declined 4% year-over-year to $288 million, with a further 200 basis point impact from the Gray Media change in the Premion partnership. Underlying AMS revenue, excluding this, declined 2% year-over-year.
  • Distribution revenue was flat year-over-year at $370 million. Heskett noted, "During the quarter, we reached a comprehensive multiyear agreement with FOX Corporation that renews station affiliations for 6 of our markets."
  • Second quarter non-GAAP expenses finished down 3% year-over-year, mainly due to operational cost-cutting. Heskett added, "At the end of the second quarter, we've achieved 80% of our target" for $90 million to $100 million in annualized core nonprogramming savings.
  • Adjusted EBITDA in Q2 decreased 14% year-over-year to $151 million, reflecting lower high-margin political and AMS revenues, partially offset by continued cost reductions.
  • The company paid $20 million in dividends in the second quarter. Cash and cash equivalents totaled $757 million at quarter end, net leverage at 2.8x.

Q&A

  • Daniel Louis Kurnos, The Benchmark Company: Asked about NBC affiliate deal stability and M&A urgency. Steib responded that network affiliate relationships remain "important and symbiotic" and that deregulation is "necessary important and coming," adding, "We are either a buyer or a seller, depending upon how the opportunities present themselves."
  • Craig Anthony Huber, Huber Research Partners: Sought examples of cost savings from AI and technology. Steib cited automation in transcription and video editing, as well as sales process enhancements. Heskett added, "We're finding really good progress on building stations of the future, which is a smaller footprint from a square footage perspective, spending potentially 80% less in CapEx utilizing the new technology and the virtual technology that is available to us and also identifying about 50% less in operating expenses by taking advantage of these opportunities."
  • Huber also probed core advertising trends for Q3. Steib described the economy as "strong but choppy" and advertising as "a little bit softer right now." Heskett noted Q3 is facing "a tougher comp with our NBC portfolio being the largest NBC affiliate group, up against the Summer Olympics last year" and estimated advertising would be "in that low doubles to mid-teens range" down year-over-year.
  • Steven Lee Cahall, Wells Fargo: Asked about the M&A market and reverse comp trends. Steib emphasized a "wide aperture" for acquisition, swap, or sale opportunities and being "dispassionate capital allocators." Heskett reported programming fee (reverse comp) trends as "flattish as we look at year-over-year trends of each of those agreements."
  • Patrick William Sholl, Barrington Research: Sought details on Premion after the end of the Gray Media reseller relationship. Steib described Premion as "a real value to local advertisers" and synergistic with TEGNA's owned and operated streaming apps, stating it is "creating a real and significant opportunity for us on both fronts."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts exhibited a neutral to slightly cautious tone, focusing on M&A urgency, advertising softness, and cost-saving specifics. Questions pressed for clarity on digital strategy, affiliate deals, and market direction.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, especially during prepared remarks. Steib stated, "I'm more confident than ever and what sets TEGNA apart." Heskett highlighted execution and discipline in cost management. In Q&A, management responses remained measured, with occasional repetition of being "disciplined" and waiting for regulatory clarity.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone shifted from curiosity about M&A and regulatory timing to a greater emphasis on advertising headwinds and digital transition. Management confidence remained steady but with more concrete details on execution and cost results.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for adjusted free cash flow ($900 million to $1.1 billion for 2024-2025) was reaffirmed in both quarters.
  • Q2 saw more detailed disclosures on cost reduction progress, with 80% of nonprogramming savings target achieved, up from 60% at the end of Q1.
  • The Q2 outlook for Q3 revenue is a sharper decline (down 18% to 20% year-over-year) compared to the Q1 Q2 outlook (down 4% to 7%).
  • Management tone remained optimistic and focused on execution, but with greater emphasis on digital revenue growth, AI-driven efficiencies, and M&A strategy flexibility.
  • Analysts shifted from regulatory and M&A focus in Q1 to probing execution on digital strategy, advertising trends, and cost savings in Q2.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing macroeconomic headwinds, advertising softness, and cautious advertiser sentiment as challenges.
  • The change in the Premion partnership with Gray Media was identified as negatively impacting AMS revenue by 200 basis points for several quarters.
  • Regulatory uncertainty regarding the FCC's top 4 prohibition rule remains a factor.
  • Management is mitigating risks through aggressive cost-cutting, digital growth initiatives, and technology deployment.

Final Takeaway

TEGNA's second quarter 2025 call emphasized continued progress on cost reductions, digital expansion, and operational transformation despite persistent advertising headwinds and ongoing regulatory flux. Management reaffirmed its free cash flow guidance, outlined disciplined capital allocation, and highlighted both the opportunities and challenges presented by industry shifts and upcoming deregulation, positioning the company to capture value in a rapidly evolving broadcast landscape.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: GFL Environmental's Green Infrastructure Partners to be recapitalized in C$4.25B deal
data.description:
GFL Environmental (NYSE:GFL) said Thursday its Green Infrastructure Partners affiliate secured an agreement with funds managed by Energy Capital Partners to recapitalize its business at an enterprise value of C$4.25B, confirming previous speculation.

Upon the deal closing, GFL (NYSE:GFL) said it will receive total gross proceeds of C$775M, of which it intends to return C$585M to its shareholders and apply C$175M to its balance sheet to fund future growth.

GFL (GFL) said it will receive C$200M of the distributed amount and own a ~30% interest in GIP valued at ~C$895M, the company said.

The company said the deal allows it to partially monetize its investment in GIP, which it co-founded in 2022 with HPS Investment Partners and GFL (GFL) CEO Patrick Dovigi.

The GIP unit provides services such as road building, paving, and site development, and operates separately from GFL's (GFL) core solid waste and environmental business.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: CLS, UBER, ANET, SMCI
data.description:
Gary Alexander upgraded Arista to Buy, citing the company’s accelerating revenue growth and expanding margins. Meanwhile, The Techie upgraded Super Micro Computer despite a post-earnings selloff, seeing value in the company’s long-term AI data center positioning. On the downside, Vinay Utham downgraded Uber Technologies (UBER) to Hold based on valuation concerns despite strong operational performance. Similarly, Danil Sereda downgraded Celestica (CLS) to Hold after its impressive run, citing limited upside at current price levels.

Upgrades

  • Arista Networks Inc. (ANET): Upgrade to Buy by Gary Alexander. The analyst’s previous valuation concerns are now outweighed by Arista’s accelerating revenue growth, expanding gross margins, and multi-year AI tailwinds.

    “After missing out on Arista's gains on valuation concerns, I acknowledge now that, for a company that is supported by multi-year AI demand acceleration trends and an expansive ~$70 billion TAM, I'm willing to pay a high price for that growth.”

  • Super Micro Computer Inc. (SMCI): Upgrade by The Techie. Despite disappointing Q4 earnings that triggered a 16% stock drop, the analyst sees the current selloff as a buying opportunity given strong AI-driven demand and undervalued valuation multiples.

    “With shares trading so low, I see this current selloff as a buying opportunity. Management is still aiming for at least $33 billion for FY2026. A revenue figure I see being fueled by this most recent AI boom going into 2H25… Supermicro holds the best position as a one-stop shop for anyone looking to revamp their data center construction.”

Downgrades

  • Uber Technologies Inc. (UBER): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Vinay Utham, CFA. Despite Uber’s strong Q2 performance with standout delivery segment growth, the analyst cites stretched valuation and weakened short-term momentum as reasons for the downgrade.

    “I have to emphasize that my rating downgrade is a pure valuation call and is not a reflection of the company’s growth prospects. UBER’s long-term growth story remains intact. It continues to be my biggest position in the portfolio for that very reason. However, given that momentum in the name has weakened and given the little-to-no upside at current levels, in my opinion, it is better for new investors to wait for a more attractive entry point.”

  • Celestica Inc. (CLS): Downgrade to Hold by Danil Sereda. Following explosive growth in the High Performance Solutions segment and significant margin expansion, the analyst sees limited upside after the stock’s massive run.

    “Initially, I thought that I'd reiterate my bullish rating on CLS again, even after the fact that the stock had exploded by over 24% since my July article on its strong Q2 beat. But it looks like a logical thing today would be downgrading to "Hold"...The beating was indeed pretty impressive, and I still think that CLS's margin expansion isn't done just yet. It may take a couple of years for this process to cement, and probably then CLS will reach its profitability peak. Right away, it's not even close to the peak in my view, and the HPS sub-segment's strength should keep pushing the consolidated margins to new heights.”

data.title: Cabaletta Bio files $240M mixed securities shelf
data.description:
• Cabaletta Bio (NASDAQ:CABA) has filed a registration statement with the SEC for a proposed $240M mixed securities offering.
  • This registration statement contains a base prospectus that covers the sale of common stock, preferred stock, debt securities, warrants and/or units in one or more offerings with a total value of up to $240M.
  • It also contains a sales agreement prospectus covering the sale of up to $150M common stock that may be issued under a sales agreement entered into with TD Securities (USA) LLC, or TD Cowen.

data.title: Caci targets over 60% free cash flow per share growth in fiscal 2026 as company advances software-defined strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: CACI International Inc (CACI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • John S. Mengucci, President and CEO, opened the call by recognizing the recent passing of Chairman Mike Daniels, highlighting his role in shaping and supporting CACI's strategy. Mengucci stated, “CACI's strong fourth quarter performance closes out another great year and underscores the strength, differentiation and resilience of our business.” He reported full-year revenue growth of 16% on an underlying basis, an EBITDA margin of 11.2%, and free cash flow of $442 million, along with free cash flow per share growth of over 16%.

  • Mengucci emphasized CACI's strategic positioning, noting investments ahead of customer needs, especially in software-defined technologies. He highlighted the Army's expansion of the TLS Manpack program, the Canadian contract for counter-UAS technology, and the NCAPS program at NASA, stating that CACI is “extremely well aligned to the environment we see today. We don't need to transform. We're already here.”

  • Jeffrey D. MacLauchlan, CFO, reported, “In the fourth quarter, we generated revenue of $2.3 billion, representing 13% year-over-year growth with 5.3% of that being organic. EBITDA margin was 11.5% in the quarter, slightly above our expectations and in line with last year. Fourth quarter adjusted diluted earnings per share of $8.40 were 27% higher than a year ago.” He also noted a $28 million tax benefit from an IRS R&D tax credit audit resolution.

Outlook

  • Management issued fiscal 2026 guidance, forecasting revenue between $9.2 billion and $9.4 billion, representing growth between 6.6% and 8.9%, with an EBITDA margin in the mid-11% range and free cash flow per share growth of over 60%. MacLauchlan specified, “Adjusted net income is expected to be between $605 million and $625 million, which translates into adjusted diluted earnings per share of between $27.13 and $28.03. We expect free cash flow of at least $710 million, which equates to free cash flow per share of $31.84.”

  • The company reiterated its confidence in its three-year financial targets, with Mengucci stating, “It is consistent with the 3-year financial targets we discussed at our Investor Day last November, which we remain highly confident in achieving.”

Financial Results

  • CACI closed fiscal year 2025 with $8.6 billion in revenue, nearly 16% total growth, and 10% organic growth, according to MacLauchlan. EBITDA margin for the year was 11.2%, and adjusted diluted earnings per share reached $26.48. Free cash flow was $442 million, reflecting a 16% increase in free cash flow per share.

  • The company completed three strategic acquisitions, repurchased $150 million in shares, and ended the year with a backlog of more than $31 billion, representing about 3.5 years of annual revenue.

Q&A

  • Scott Stephen Mikus, Melius Research, raised concerns about the pipeline's composition and competitive dynamics for new business. Mengucci responded, “We serve 7 markets. We're very, very focused, and we retool the entire company around understanding what is a value bid and what is not value bid...The majority of that would be new work to CACI and well over half of that is going to be new customer work as well.”

  • Mikus also inquired about the EITaaS program ceiling reduction. Mengucci clarified, “EITaaS is a 10-year program, and the ceiling was reduced from $5.7 billion to $5 billion. It doesn't change a thing for this company...there's no impact to guidance. There's no reduction in revenue. There's no reduction in margin or any of our 3-year targets.”

  • Colin Michael Canfield, Cantor, asked about guidance sensitivity to government budget timing. Mengucci explained, “If the government fiscal year '26 CR is shorter and the budget gets passed sooner and funding remains steady, then we could see us towards the higher end.”

  • Gavin Eric Parsons, UBS, questioned the award environment and slowdowns. Mengucci acknowledged modest impacts but stated, “Awards are lumpy...But as I mentioned earlier, I think as the procurement bandwidth gets a little tighter, we believe that could result in a few other outcomes, one being that the current work we have gets extended.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on pipeline quality, budget sensitivity, margin trajectory, and contract changes, with a tone that was neutral to slightly positive, seeking clarification on guidance robustness and strategic focus.

  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone throughout, emphasizing resilience and alignment with customer priorities. Phrases such as “we are extremely well aligned to the environment we see today” and “we remain highly confident in achieving” indicated a high level of confidence.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's sentiment remained positive and consistent, with analysts maintaining a similar level of inquiry and cautious optimism.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company’s Q4 performance built on momentum from Q3, maintaining strong revenue and margin growth. The guidance for fiscal 2026 projects slightly lower revenue growth than the prior year’s 16%, but free cash flow per share growth is explicitly targeted at over 60% for the upcoming year, a significant increase from over 16% growth previously reported.

  • Strategic focus on software-defined solutions, resilience, and pipeline quality remained consistent. Management’s tone was comparably confident, and analysts continued to focus on pipeline, margins, and the impact of government funding cycles.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted the possibility of a government continuing resolution impacting the timing of revenue, particularly for software-defined technology, but expressed confidence in managing such scenarios.

  • Supply chain and manufacturing constraints were mentioned regarding space optical terminals, but Mengucci asserted that “it's not an underlying technology issue.”

  • The company is closely monitoring the government fiscal year 2026 budget process but does not foresee material business impacts under a CR.

Final Takeaway

CACI's leadership emphasizes that the company's robust results and strong fiscal year 2026 guidance are the outcome of years of intentional strategic execution. With a clear focus on software-defined capabilities, disciplined capital deployment, and alignment to enduring national security priorities, CACI asserts confidence in delivering substantial free cash flow per share growth and continued shareholder value in the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Stocks cut gains, trade mixed as investors weigh Trump tariffs, Russia-Ukraine progress
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages on Thursday gave up a positive open to trade mixed as a boost to sentiment from U.S. President Donald Trump's chip tariff exemptions and progress on Russia-Ukraine de-escalation petered out.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) seesawed, giving up an advance of as much as +0.7%, last -0.1% in afternoon trade. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was last -0.7%. 

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.3%, boosted by a post-earnings climb in AppLovin (APP) and DoorDash (DASH), and extended gains in tech titan Apple (AAPL). The iPhone-maker on Wednesday confirmed a new $100B investment in the U.S.

The tech-heavy index was also boosted by several chip stocks, as investors took heart from Trump's vow to exempt semiconductor manufacturers from a planned 100% tariff on imports if they made their products in the United States.

President Trump “won’t charge tariffs while you’re building in the country, but if you’re not building in the country, 100% tariffs,” Secretary of Commerce Howard Lutnick told Fox Business in the morning.

Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), the world's largest contract chipmaker, clinched a record high on Thursday after Taiwan said TSM will not have to pay the 100% tariff.

Speaking of tariffs, reciprocal barriers that had been postponed twice finally went into effect against U.S. trading partners across the world on Wednesday, bringing the average tariff rate to levels not seen since World War II.   

“Is this the new global tariff regime? Not quite yet. While some countries are still negotiating with the U.S., America’s new tariff regime entered into effect at midnight, increasing the effective U.S. tariff level from just under 3% at the beginning of the year to just over 15%,” said Mohamed A. El-Erian, chief economic adviser at Allianz and former CEO of PIMCO.

“Up to this point, many companies appear to have used inventories and profit margins as buffers, as well as pressure on suppliers, while waiting to see what would finally emerge. Now, they must make more challenging, longer-term decisions about whether and how much to adjust prices,” El-Erian added. 

Also grabbing headlines were signs of progress on a truce between Russia and Ukraine.

"We had some very good talks with President Putin today, and there's a very good chance that we could be ending the round — the end of that road. There's a good chance that there will be a meeting very soon," Trump told reporters on Wednesday.

Meanwhile, The New York Times reported that Trump was planning to hold in-person meetings with Russian and Ukrainian leaders Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelenskyy as soon as next week. 

Turning to Thursday's economic calendar, initial jobless claims jumped more than expected, while continuing claims rose to their highest mark since 2021.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was little changed at 4.23%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 1 basis point to 3.73%. Traders will be keeping an eye on an upcoming $25B 30-year Treasury bond auction.
